obligations: Guarantee of obligations: FA §21, Exhibits B-1/B-2
restrictions: No territorial development/sales quotas applicable

obligations: Make all rental payments to CFA
Comply with prime lease/ground lease terms
Accept security interest in business equipment
Personal guarantee if assigned to entity
rights: CFA provides extended payment terms for pre-opening
Equipment rental at $750–$5,000/mo based on fair market value
restrictions: Cannot obtain external financing for franchise (CFA model eliminates need)
Default: termination of lease, franchise, full payment due
triggers: Default on lease/sublease/equipment rental → termination + full liability
Holdover after lease expiration → double rent as liquidated damages
Failure to perform lease obligations → CFA may perform + charge costs + interest

obligations: Complete 5-week initial training program before opening
Train all personnel using CFA materials
Participate in mandatory seminars/conferences if required by CFA
Participate in mandatory promotional campaigns (potentially at own cost)
rights: CFA pays for initial training cost
CFA provides monthly fee calculation report
CFA administers cash handling and accounting system
restrictions: Must use POS system provided by CFA
CFA has independent access to POS data
Cannot use non-CFA-approved advertising materials

obligations: Food trucks only at CFA-registered and approved locations
rights: May solicit/accept orders from outside trading area (subject to requirements)
restrictions: No exclusive territory, express or implied
No right to exclude other CFA locations
Cannot use unapproved distribution channels
Cannot relocate — only CFA can decide relocation
Must terminate existing FA and pay new fee for relocation

obligations: Use marks only in connection with franchised restaurant operation
Notify CFA immediately of possible infringement
Cease all trademark use immediately upon termination
restrictions: Cannot use marks outside of franchise agreement scope
No entitlement to damages/costs recovered in infringement proceedings

obligations: Acknowledge access to trade secrets and proprietary information
Never publish or disclose trade secrets (perpetual for GA trade secrets; 2 years post-term for other confidential info)
Cease all patent/invention use upon termination
restrictions: Cannot use proprietary info outside approved purposes
No entitlement to damages/costs recovered in patent proceedings

obligations: Devote full time and personal best efforts
If entity: own 100%, be sole director/manager/officer, personal guarantee
restrictions: Only individuals may sign initial FA
Entity name cannot contain 'Chick-fil-A', 'CFA', or derogatory terms
Formation documents must recite transfer restriction

obligations: Request written permission for any non-approved products
restrictions: Only CFA-approved products may be sold
Premises used exclusively for CFA restaurant operations
CFA can add required products at any time
